description_long: This is a heavy-duty, double-headed hammer with a robust, cylindrical metal head featuring pointed spikes on both ends. The head is flanked by two large, flat striking surfaces, while the central axis of the head is reinforced with a triangular support structure. The handle is long, straight, and textured with a series of pronounced ridges, likely for enhanced grip and control. Both the top and bottom of the hammer are capped with sharp, conical spikes, giving it an aggressive, industrial appearance. The entire object is finished in a dark, metallic color, suggesting it is constructed primarily from steel or a similar alloy.
description: A dark, double-headed metal hammer with pointed spikes on both ends and a ridged, textured handle for grip.
description_view_0: The hammer is seen at an angle, clearly showing both the double striking heads and the pointed spikes at each end.
description_view_1: This top-down view emphasizes the cylindrical shape of the hammer's head and the alignment of the spikes.
description_view_2: A mirrored angle to view 0, highlighting the triangular support and the symmetry of the hammer's design.
description_view_3: The hammer is viewed from the side, showcasing the full length of the ridged handle and the sharp spike at the base.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical steel handle with pronounced ridges for grip enhancement; appears textured and robust.
attribute_head_type: Double-headed sledge-type hammer with cylindrical striking faces and sharp conical spikes on both ends; reinforced with a triangular support structure.
